methods.description: The aquatic model in the software PREDO 3 was used to assess the extent to which the committed dose from each of the investigated radionuclides results from exposure through the marine food chain. The model includes radionuclide transfer between different geographical regions, from the water column to marine biota, and between different trophic levels within the food web, ultimately leading to human exposure through intake of marine-origin food.

steps.description: The PREDO model for the marine environment at the Ringhals nuclear power plant was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use PREDO model for marine environment simulation

steps.description: The annual dose after 100 years of release of 1 Bq/year is calculated and then normalized to actual releases.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and normalize annual dose after 100 years

steps.description: The implemented CF for each of the studied elements (Ni, Ru, and Sb) was varied by a factor of 10 and 1/10 respectively, to assess dose proportionality to the CF.
steps.searchable_summary: vary CF by factor of 10 and 1/10 to assess dose proportionality
inputs: CF values for Ni, Ru, Sb

methods.description: Seawater samples were collected and analyzed to determine natural background concentrations of Ni, Ru, and Sb, as well as gamma and beta-emitting radionuclides.

steps.description: Samples were collected using a water sampler and immediately poured into 5L cans and refrigerated under dark conditions.
Natural background concentrations of Ni, Ru, and Sb were determined using ICP-MS with the standard addition method.
outputs: background concentrations of Ni, Ru, Sb

steps.description: Natural background concentrations of gamma-emitting radionuclides were assessed using a high-purity germanium coaxial detector.
steps.searchable_summary: assess gamma-emitting radionuclides using germanium detector

steps.description: Natural background concentrations of beta-emitting radionuclides were assessed using a Liquid Scintillation Counter.
steps.searchable_summary: assess beta-emitting radionuclides using scintillation counter

methods.description: Activity measurements were conducted on samples with added radionuclides, using different methods for different radionuclides due to their decay characteristics.

steps.description: Samples were filtered through polycarbonate membrane filters and rinsed with sterile filtered seawater before activity measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and rinse samples before activity measurement

steps.description: The activity of 125 Sb was assessed directly by gamma counting using a high-purity germanium coaxial detector.
steps.searchable_summary: assess 125 Sb activity using gamma counting

steps.description: The activity of 106 Ru was measured indirectly through gamma spectrometry of 106 Rh.
steps.searchable_summary: measure 106 Ru activity via 106 Rh gamma spectrometry

steps.description: The activity adsorbed in the culture flasks was measured and geometry corrections were made using EFFTRAN.
steps.searchable_summary: measure flask activity and apply geometry corrections
